purpose: Lifecycle registry for active Theseus surfaces so scripts, reports, generated artifacts, compatibility wrappers, and docs do not accumulate without an owner.

registry_evolution_contract.purpose: The registry is Theseus' current self-model. Autonomous or human changes should improve registered surfaces first, and only create new surfaces when the registry explains the need, owner, lifecycle, and verification path.

rules.rule: Every active source, config, benchmark card, and operational doc must be matched by a registry surface before it can be treated as part of Theseus.
Before adding a new vN, seed, current, after, smoke, or one-off lane, update the canonical registered surface or explain the successor relationship in the registry.
rules.enforcement: source duplicate families produce registry governance violations and ATTD/control-plane maintenance work

rules.rule: The materialized registry is the current self-model; every routable implementation must have accepted minimal route evidence while supporting and retained reports remain non-authorizing history.
rules.enforcement: source-bound, TTL-bound, or current-invocation route receipts must pass; supporting outputs do not expire routes

rules.rule: Generated caches, pycache, build products, runtime payloads, and installer binaries must not live in source paths as if they were source.
A replacement surface must either supersede, deprecate, or explicitly coexist with the previous surface; do not leave old and new systems competing silently.
rules.enforcement: deprecated/retained surfaces need replacement, retained reason, or cleanup policy

rules.rule: Evidence, benchmark calibration records, training rows, and release artifacts are not deleted blindly; heavy/generated artifacts move through retention or GC manifests with pointers.
rules.enforcement: cleanup tools must emit manifests and archive pointers for moved artifacts

rules.rule: Every canonical implementation must bind to a stable abstraction contract, and every live abstraction must name a canonical live implementation.
Autonomous roadmap work must have a steward charter, an active project work contract, event-taint coverage, module DoD cards, and steward decisions before execution.

abstraction_registry_contract.purpose: Stable contract layer for Theseus. Abstractions define what the system needs to remain true while implementations underneath can be swapped, improved, deprecated, or compared.
rules: Code and training work should improve or replace an implementation bound to an existing abstraction before introducing a new abstraction.
A new abstraction is allowed only when no existing contract can honestly own the behavior.
Implementations may compete under one abstraction, but only one live implementation may be canonical unless the abstraction declares a split route.
Benchmarks, tools, VCM, STS, Hive, and model substrates must bind through abstractions before they can be claimed as part of Theseus' active identity.
Routers, templates, deterministic tools, and learned generators may coexist only when their implementation type and evidence role are explicit.

route_evidence_contract.purpose: Separate the minimal current receipts that authorize a route from supporting and retained evidence. Static gates invalidate when their owning source changes; volatile state uses an explicit TTL; the registry itself is evaluated in the current invocation.
rules: Supporting reports do not expire a route merely because time passed.
Every routable implementation names a minimal independently evaluated route receipt.
Source-bound evidence becomes stale when an explicitly named implementation source changes after the receipt.
A fresh receipt may still be rejected by its acceptance predicate.
Historical and negative evidence stays retained and cannot authorize current routing.

claim_boundaries.learned_generation: Only implementations whose contract role is learned_generation and whose independent candidate-integrity evidence confirms learned prompt/signature-conditioned output may claim learned code generation.
claim_boundaries.runtime_serving: Runtime-serving implementations must report zero external inference and current local-serving evidence.
claim_boundaries.public_calibration: Public benchmark surfaces are measurement-only and are not training data sources.

evidence_loss.cause: A non-executing plan invocation overwrote the ignored local candidate JSONL before dry-run publication was made non-mutating.
evidence_loss.recovery: Exact evaluation-only replay was attempted and denied because the bound checkpoint receipt is incomplete; the completeness gate was not weakened.
evidence_loss.claim_effect: The historical candidate-integrity check is false and cannot authorize promotion; immutable checkpoint, blind-flow, negative replay, and rollback evidence remain bound.

criteria: candidate integrity or blind information-flow gate becomes non-GREEN
checkpoint or evidence content binding changes without requalification
model-only private verifier pass returns to zero on the frozen split

claim_boundary.supported_claim: Correcting the split-vocabulary source offset raises independently integrity-verified canonical body-only candidates from 38/88 to 57/87 and reduces inert stubs from 50 to 30, but model-only behavior remains 0/24 and training provenance remains incomplete. Matched emitted-plan, global latent-plan, sigmoid slot-attention, and categorical slot-attention arms learn source-only plan targets but remain 0/24 exact. Under categorical slots, semantic plan F1 and body loss beat shuffled labels while verifier reward regresses below shuffled and decode cost rises; it is NOT_ADOPTED. The successor remains contained, body-only, and non-routable.

duplicate_family_classifications.successor_policy: package manifests are permitted only as one pinned dependency environment per registered verifier toolchain; they do not define competing product or model implementations
lockfiles must remain paired with a registered verifier toolchain manifest and are dependency evidence rather than implementation successors
new neural configs must extend the canonical comparator or declare a successor in this registry
do not create new neural_seed lanes without deprecating or extending the canonical comparator
old broad_private/body-template outputs may inform residuals but cannot silently feed promotion
new code_lm helpers must be imported by the canonical generator/fanout path or registered separately
edge_contract variants must be consumed by the replay/receiver contract or deprecated
VCM features must show same-surface lift before entering a promotion path
hive operations scripts must remain registered under install/operator surfaces
native voice work is retained but must not create new model-training lanes under this goal
post_v4 scripts may supply audit context but promotion must flow through current closure/replay gates
pressure runner variants must write into existing residual/candidate replay contracts
VCM reference variants must stay under docs/reference/virtual_context_memory_v1.0 unless a successor packet is explicitly registered
report families are generated evidence history; latest-view reports stay declared by surfaces and bulky variants must move through retention manifests rather than becoming new source lanes

route_evidence_contracts.reason: The registry computes its own implementation health in the current invocation; requiring its previous output would create a circular stale-self dependency.
The implementation is retained or evidence-only and cannot be selected as a route.

artifact_identity.provenance_policy: source/config/dependency/evidence paths must be registry-visible before claims are credited
artifact_identity.mutable_instance_policy: online adaptation is allowed only inside the field adaptation envelope or it becomes a new implementation revision

observability_binding.privacy_boundary: structured receipts and artifact references only; no private chain-of-thought required

content_binding.artifact_hash_policy: bind implementation revision to source/model/prompt/configuration artifact hashes before qualification
content_binding.manifest_hash_policy: bind implementation manifest hash and treat manifest edits as qualification-relevant changes
content_binding.contract_hash_policy: bind the exact field contract/profile revision targeted by this implementation
content_binding.dependency_closure_hash_policy: bind dependency closure digest, including tools, models, prompts, config, and runtime dependencies when applicable
content_binding.evidence_bundle_hash_policy: bind evidence bundles by hash; report paths alone are not authorization identity

route_binding.caller_context_binding: route validation binds caller identity/request class/purpose and does not inherit implementation privilege
route_binding.request_context_hash_policy: material request context is receipt-bound or explicitly marked out of scope
route_binding.fallback_binding: fallback is a separately qualified route and never counted as learned capability evidence

claim_binding.qualification_claims: claims must cite exact artifact, manifest, contract, profile, dependency, state relation, evaluator policy, and evidence bundle identities
claim_binding.assumptions: claim assumptions are explicit; missing values are unknown, not independent

lease_binding.state_binding: lease binds state relation/migration path and route-local state compatibility

adaptation_binding.epoch_token_policy: online adaptation requires a sealed epoch token; breach seals and revokes it
adaptation_binding.updater_revision_policy: adaptation updater revision is pinned and qualification-relevant
adaptation_binding.data_receipts_policy: adaptation inputs require approved data receipts and provenance tags
adaptation_binding.sentinel_binding: runtime sentinels and breach behavior are declared before adaptation starts
adaptation_binding.journal_policy: adaptation journal commits deltas, receipts, sentinels, and rollback/containment evidence
adaptation_binding.baseline_binding: qualified baseline is bound for comparison, rollback, or containment

migration_binding.state_relation_binding: state relation hash/class is bound before claiming compatibility
migration_binding.rehearsal_required: durable state/effect migration requires rehearsal evidence or explicit non-solvent containment
